exercises.title: Collaboration Health Check
exercises.description: Assess your team's collaboration across five dimensions: information sharing, help-seeking, cross-team work, accountability, and trust.
hints: Ask team members for their independent ratings
Compare your ratings to theirs — the gaps reveal blind spots

solution.code:
Collaboration Health Check:

1. Information Sharing (1-5)
   'Information is shared openly and proactively'
   Rating: ___
   Evidence: ___________

2. Help-Seeking (1-5)
   'People ask for help without fear of judgment'
   Rating: ___
   Evidence: ___________

3. Cross-Team Work (1-5)
   'We collaborate regularly with other teams'
   Rating: ___
   Evidence: ___________

4. Accountability (1-5)
   'We hold each other accountable for commitments'
   Rating: ___
   Evidence: ___________

5. Trust (1-5)
   'We trust each other to do quality work'
   Rating: ___
   Evidence: ___________

Lowest dimension: ___________
Action to improve: ___________
Review in 30 days: ___________

exercises.title: Cross-Functional Collaboration Plan
exercises.description: Design a collaboration plan for a cross-functional project. Define shared goals, roles, communication, and conflict resolution.
hints: Start with the shared goal — what does success look like for everyone?
Use the RAPID framework for decision-making roles
Define communication channels and meeting cadence upfront

solution.code:
Cross-Functional Collaboration Plan:

Project: ___________
Shared goal: ___________
Timeline: ___________

Roles (RAPID):
R — Recommend: [Team/Person]
A — Agree: [Team/Person]
I — Input: [Teams/People]
P — Perform: [Teams]
D — Decide: [One Person]

Communication:
- Channel: ___________
- Sync meetings: [frequency, duration, attendees]
- Async updates: [frequency, format]
- Decision documentation: [where decisions are recorded]

Conflict resolution:
- Process: [how disagreements are handled]
- Escalation: [who resolves if parties can't agree]

Success metrics:
- How will we know it worked? ___________
- Review date: ___________

exercises.title: Information Sharing Audit
exercises.description: Identify one piece of information your team needs but doesn't have access to. Design a sharing mechanism to fix it.
hints: Ask: 'What information do you wish you had?'
Consider: status dashboards, meeting notes, decision logs, customer feedback
Make it accessible by default, not on request

solution.code:
Information Sharing Fix:

Information gap: ___________
Who needs it: ___________
Why they don't have it: ___________

Sharing mechanism:
- Format: [dashboard, shared doc, weekly email, Slack channel]
- Frequency: [real-time, daily, weekly]
- Owner: [who maintains it]
- Access: [who can view/edit]

Example:
Information gap: Customer support ticket trends
Who needs it: Product team
Why: They only see feature requests, not support pain points
Mechanism: Weekly summary in shared Slack channel, owned by support lead
Frequency: Every Monday
Access: Product and support channels
